records.ety: [L. contemptus, fr. contemnere: cf. OF. contempt. See Contemn.]

defs: The act of contemning or despising; the feeling with which one regards that which is esteemed mean, vile, or worthless; disdain; scorn.
quotes: Nothing, says Longinus, can be great, the contempt of which is great.

defs: The state of being despised; disgrace; shame.
quotes: Contempt and begarry hangs upon thy back.

defs: An act or expression denoting contempt.
quotes: The contempt and anger of his lip.

defs: Disobedience of the rules, orders, or process of a court of justice, or of rules or orders of a legislative body; disorderly, contemptuous, or insolent language or behavior in presence of a court, tending to disturb its proceedings, or impair the respect due to its authority.
notes: ☞ Contempt is in some jurisdictions extended so as to include publications reflecting injuriously on a court of justice, or commenting unfairly on pending proceedings; in other jurisdictions the courts are prohibited by statute or by the constitution from thus exercising this process.
